steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage makes general, timeless statements about programming languages using consistent present tense: they 'have a clear difference,' one 'is object-oriented,' and both 'require' technical knowledge. The blank verb must match this present-tense pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'struggle': present tense, consistent with the passage's timeless generalizations. ✓
  • Choice B — past-tense form: shifts to past tense, contradicting the general present-tense context. ✗
  • Choice C — past-tense form: same problem — past tense breaks the pattern. ✗
  • Choice D — past-tense form: also past tense, inconsistent with the surrounding verbs. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
